pr.title: add PUSH_ATTRS macro for fighter chara OnLoad, works on the first 8 o…
pr.author: snuffysasa

summary: Introduced a reusable PUSH_ATTRS(ft, attributeName) macro in fighter.h for fighter character OnLoad routines that copy x10C_ftData->ext_attr into x2D8_specialAttributes2 and assign x2D4_specialAttributes. The PR used it across multiple fighters, moved several OnLoad/death routines from asm to C for Fox, Link, and Mewtwo, added ftlink.c to obj_files.mk, cleaned up Pikachu code/symbol names, and renamed several clone-specific OnLoad helpers such as ftCFalcon_OnLoadForGanon, ftFox_OnLoadForFalco, ftLink_OnLoadForCLink, ftMario_OnLoadForDrMario, ftKoopa_OnLoadForGKoopa, and ftMars_OnLoadForRoy. Author noted DOL was OK; there was no review discussion in the slice.
what_changed: Added PUSH_ATTRS(ft, attributeName) in src/melee/ft/fighter.h. The macro saves x2D8_specialAttributes2, casts x10C_ftData->ext_attr to the requested attribute struct, copies that struct into x2D8_specialAttributes2, and stores the backup pointer into x2D4_specialAttributes.

Replaced repeated hand-written attribute-copy boilerplate in multiple character OnLoad and clone-OnLoad routines with PUSH_ATTRS, including Captain/Falcon, Mars/Marth, MasterHand, Ness, Pikachu, Fox, Koopa, Link, Mario, Mewtwo, Peach, Sandbag, and Zako Boy/Girl files according to the diff matches.

Matched or moved ftFox_OnLoad, ftLink_OnLoad/related Link routines, and ftMewtwo_OnLoad out of asm: large blocks were deleted from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftfox.s, ftlink.s, and ftmewtwo.s while corresponding C files gained implementations.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftlink.c.o to obj_files.mk so the new Link C translation unit participates in the text object build.

Updated include/functions.h to replace several address-style names/comments with clearer character-specific names, including ftCFalcon_OnLoadForGanon, ftFox_OnLoadForFalco, ftLink_OnLoadForCLink, ftMario_OnLoadForDrMario, ftKoopa_OnLoadForGKoopa, ftMars_OnLoadForRoy, and Pikachu helper names.

Updated asm/melee/ft/ftdata.s function pointer tables to use renamed Pikachu symbols such as ftPikachu_UnkHeldItemAction_801244B8, ftPikachu_80124598, ftPikachu_UnkHeldItem_801245E0, ftPikachu_80124628, ftPikachu_8012467C, ftPikachu_801246C0, ftPikachu_801247A8, ftPikachu_801247EC, and ftPikachu_ScaleYAttributesArray_80124704.
Expanded fighter.h struct coverage around Link-related fighter fields in the x222C through x2248 region, based on the diff excerpt showing named u32 members added.
smart_moves: Factored a common OnLoad pattern into a macro instead of duplicating per-fighter pointer casts and struct assignment code, reducing opportunities for mismatched local-variable ordering while preserving the exact attribute struct type at each call site.

Kept the macro type-explicit: each call passes the concrete attribute struct name, making the generated copy size follow the fighter-specific ext_attr struct instead of relying on an untyped memcpy-like helper.
Used clone-specific names for shared OnLoad helpers, e.g. ftCFalcon_OnLoadForGanon and ftLink_OnLoadForCLink, which records both the implementation owner and the consumer clone.
Renamed function pointer table entries together with the functions, preventing ftdata.s from retaining stale func_ address names after C-side cleanup.
Preserved unknown/uncertain semantics in Pikachu names by using address suffixes and Unk terms rather than overclaiming behavior.
decomp_lessons: Many fighter OnLoad routines perform the same special-attribute push: copy x10C_ftData->ext_attr into x2D8_specialAttributes2 and set x2D4_specialAttributes to that storage. Reuse PUSH_ATTRS when decompiling similar character OnLoad code.

For clone characters, prefer names that describe the source character implementation and target clone usage: ftBase_OnLoadForClone is more searchable than a raw func_ address and avoids implying the helper belongs only to the clone.

When a symbol appears in data tables such as ft_OnItemPickup, ft_OnItemInvisible, ft_OnItemVisible, ft_OnItemRelease, ft_OnItemCatch, and knockback/item callback tables, rename the table entries in the same PR as the function declarations/definitions.
If exact semantics remain unclear, keep the address suffix in the new name, e.g. ftPikachu_80124598 or ftPikachu_ScaleYAttributesArray_80124704, to preserve traceability back to the original symbol.
Build integration matters for asm-to-C migration: after adding a new C translation unit such as ftlink.c, update obj_files.mk and remove only the corresponding asm ranges.
naming_conventions: PUSH_ATTRS(ft, attributeName) is an all-caps macro name and takes the fighter pointer plus a concrete attribute struct type.

Clone OnLoad helpers use ft<sourceCharacter>_OnLoadFor<cloneCharacter>, as shown by ftCFalcon_OnLoadForGanon, ftFox_OnLoadForFalco, ftLink_OnLoadForCLink, ftMario_OnLoadForDrMario, ftKoopa_OnLoadForGKoopa, and ftMars_OnLoadForRoy.
Character OnLoad routines use ft<Character>_OnLoad, e.g. ftFox_OnLoad, ftLink_OnLoad, and ftMewtwo_OnLoad.

Pikachu cleanup names use ftPikachu_ prefixes and retain address suffixes for unclear callbacks, e.g. ftPikachu_80124628, while partially inferred behavior gets names such as ftPikachu_UnkHeldItemAction_801244B8.
The PR uses project-style fighter struct field names such as x10C_ftData, x2D8_specialAttributes2, and x2D4_specialAttributes rather than inventing new semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The deleted asm snippets show the common matching pattern: load ext_attr from fighter data, copy a fixed-size attribute struct with a counted bdnz loop, then store the destination pointer into x2D4_specialAttributes.
PUSH_ATTRS appears designed to emit compatible code for multiple OnLoad functions by forcing the compiler to perform a struct assignment of the exact attribute type.
The PR removed full asm bodies for ftFox_OnLoad, ftMewtwo_OnLoad, and substantial Link routines after matching them in C.
Function pointer table symbol renames in ftdata.s are low-risk matching changes because they alter labels/references without changing emitted data values.
The author explicitly stated 'DOL is OK', indicating the macro-based C replacements and symbol cleanups preserved the expected binary output for this PR.
follow_up_queries: Where else are fighter OnLoad routines still open-coded instead of using PUSH_ATTRS?
Which later PRs refined the PUSH_ATTRS macro or replaced it with an inline/helper approach?
Were the Link x222C-x2248 fields later given semantic names?
Did later Pikachu decomp work resolve the UnkHeldItem callback names introduced here?
Which remaining clone OnLoad helpers still use func_ address names after PR 383?
